name: Do I have to build some kind of custom Alexa or Google Assistant “skill” for my contracting business to benefit from voice search?
description: You don’t need custom Alexa or Google Assistant skills for local voice visibility. Assistants mainly rely on your Google/Apple listings, website content, schema

mainEntity.name: Do I have to build some kind of custom Alexa or Google Assistant “skill” for my contracting business to benefit from voice search?

acceptedAnswer.text:
No, you don’t need to build a custom Alexa or Google Assistant “skill” to benefit from voice search as a contractor. For most local home‑service businesses, assistants rely on existing web data and listings.

Custom skills are optional and usually only make sense for larger or specialized brands.

Key factors:
- Local voice queries typically pull from Google Business Profile, maps, and FAQs.
- Building and maintaining a skill requires development effort and ongoing updates.
- The return is often limited compared to strengthening basic local SEO.
- Schema markup and reviews offer higher impact for less complexity.

Focus on being the best‑structured, most trusted answer in the data assistants already use. Consider custom skills only if you have a clear unique interaction in mind.

For most contractors, fundamentals beat custom voice apps by a wide margin.
acceptedAnswer.description: You don’t need custom Alexa or Google Assistant skills for local voice visibility. Assistants mainly rely on your Google/Apple listings, website content, schema, and reviews. For most contractors, optimizing these basics delivers far more value than building a custom skill.
